message:
fix(net): reject empty recv_from buffers and accept LF-only HTTP headers in websocket handshake

Three related network-layer hardening changes:

UDP (src/net/udp.rs):
  On non-WASM targets, poll_recv_from now returns InvalidInput immediately
  when passed an empty buffer instead of silently consuming the next datagram
  from the kernel and returning zero bytes. This prevents data loss: callers
  who accidentally pass &mut [] will get a clear error and the datagram
  remains in the socket's receive queue for a subsequent correctly-buffered
  read. Test added exercising the error path and verifying the datagram
  survives.

Unix datagram (src/net/unix/datagram.rs):
  Identical empty-buffer guard for UnixDatagram::recv_from, with the same
  rationale — prevent silent datagram consumption on zero-length buffers.
  Test added mirroring the UDP test.

WebSocket client & handshake:
  read_http_response (client.rs) now recognises bare-LF (\n\n) header
  terminators in addition to CRLF (\r\n\r\n). Some proxies and embedded
  servers emit LF-only responses; previously these caused an infinite read
  loop because the 4-byte CRLF window never matched. The handshake parser
  (handshake.rs) already handled both terminators but used an if-else chain;
  refactored to a chained map_or_else for consistency with the client-side
  fix. Test added for LF-only header parsing with trailing frame bytes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
